event: Bi Wan enfeoffed at Wei after conquest of Huo, Geng, and Wei
Duke Xian of Jin died; four sons struggled for succession, Jin in chaos
Wei Wuzi followed Prince Chong'er into exile
Chong'er returned, became Duke Wen of Jin; Wuzi inherited Wei fief
Wei Jiang punished Yang Gan for disorder at feudal gathering
Duke Dao praised Wei Jiang: 'In eight years, nine gatherings of feudal lords'
Wei Xianzi administered state affairs; six ministers grew powerful
Confucius became minister of Lu
Zhao Jianzi, Han, and Wei attacked Fan and Zhonghang clans
Fortified Shaoliang
Sent Zi Ji to besiege Fan and Pang
Attacked Qin, built Linjin and Yuanli
Attacked Zhongshan, left Zi Ji to guard it
Wei, Zhao, and Han recognized as feudal lords
Qin attacked Wei, reached Yanghu
Guo Mountain collapsed, blocked the Yellow River
Attacked Zheng, fortified Suanzao
Qi attacked and took Xiangling
Qin invaded Yinjin
Marquis Wen died, Zi Ji succeeded as Marquis Wu
Wei, Han, and Zhao divided Jin territory, ended Jin line
Marquis Wu died, son succeeded as King Hui
Wei defeated Han at Maling, defeated Zhao at Huai
Defeated Han at Hui; fought Qin at Shaoliang, captured
Qin took Shaoliang; Wei besieged Handan
Took Handan; Qi sent Tian Ji and Sun Bin to save Zhao, Wei defeated at Guiling
Returned Handan to Zhao, made alliance
Wei attacked Zhao; Qi sent Sun Bin; Wei defeated at Maling, Crown Prince Shen captured, General Pang Juan killed
Qin, Zhao, Qi attacked Wei; Shang Yang captured Prince Ang; Wei moved capital to Daliang
King Hui died, King Xiang succeeded
Qin defeated Wei army of 45,000 at Diaoyin
Wei ceded Shang Commandery to Qin
Five states attacked Qin together, failed
Wei, Qi, and Han defeated Qin at Hangu Pass
Helped Han attack Qin; Bai Qi defeated Wei army of 240,000 at Yique
Ceded 400 li of Hedong to Qin
Qin took 61 cities from Wei
Qin defeated Wei, Han, Zhao, killed 150,000
Qin besieged Handan; Lord Xinling saved Zhao
Lord Xinling led five states against Qin, defeated Meng Ao
King Anxi died, Crown Prince Zeng succeeded as King Jingmin
Qin took 20 cities, established Dong Commandery
Qin took Chaoge
King Jingmin died, King Jia succeeded
Yan Crown Prince Dan sent Jing Ke to assassinate Qin King
Qin flooded Daliang, captured King Jia, Wei destroyed
